channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world

item.title: Speculation grows that Austrian far right leader Herbert Kickl will be asked to form a government
item.description: VIENNA (AP) — Austrian President Alexander Van der Bellen on Sunday announced that he would meet with far-right politician Herbert Kickl as speculation grows that he will ask the Freedom Party leader to form a government.
item.dc:creator: STEPHANIE LIECHTENSTEIN, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Heavy snow brings widespread disruption across the UK and Germany
item.description: LONDON (AP) — Heavy snow and freezing rain brought widespread disruption across Europe on Sunday, particularly in the U.K. and Germany, with several major airports forced to suspend flights. With the weather set to stay inclement on Sunday in the U.K.
item.dc:creator: PAN PYLAS and MONIKA SCISLOWSKA, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: South Korean protesters call for President Yoon’s arrest as deadline nears
item.description: Protesters in South Korea are calling for President Yoon Suk Yeol to be arrested as a warrant expires at midnight on Monday.
item.dc:creator: Lauren Irwin, The Hill

item.title: Ukraine will ask allies to boost its air defenses at a meeting in Germany, Zelenskyy says
item.description: Ukraine's President Volodymyr Zelenskyy says he will again call on allies to boost his country's air defenses at a meeting this week in Germany.
item.dc:creator: Volodymr Yurchuk And Elise Morton, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Trump appears with Italian Prime Minister Meloni at his Florida club
item.description: President-elect Donald Trump made an appearance with Italian Prime Minister Giorgia Meloni, who was visiting his Mar-a-Lago club.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: South Korean protesters brave cold to demand Yoon's ouster as detention deadline looms
item.description: Hundreds of South Koreans, bundled up against freezing temperatures and snow, rallied overnight near the residence of impeached President Yoon Suk Yeol, calling for his ouster and arrest, as authorities prepared to renew their efforts to detain him over his short-lived martial law decree.
item.dc:creator: Kim Tong-Hyung, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Israeli airstrikes in Gaza kill at least 21, hospital workers say, as talks underway
item.description: DEIR AL-BALAH, Gaza Strip (AP) — Israeli airstrikes killed at least 21 people, including at least two children, on Saturday in Gaza, hospital staff said, while a new effort at ceasefire talks was underway in Qatar after nearly 15 months of war.
item.dc:creator: WAFAA SHURAFA, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Austrian Chancellor Nehammer says he will resign after talks on forming a new government fail
item.description: VIENNA (AP) — Austrian Chancellor Karl Nehammer said Saturday he will resign in the coming days after talks on forming a new government failed a second time.
item.dc:creator: STEPHANIE LIECHTENSTEIN, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Central American troops arrive in Haiti to reinforce mission to restore order
item.description: PORT-AU-PRINCE, Haiti (AP) — About 150 military police officers from Central America have arrived in Haiti to reinforce the embattled government's fight against violent gangs that have upended daily life for millions in the Caribbean country.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Royal family pays tribute to Prince William, Harry's nanny who lost stepson in New Orleans attack
item.description: Prince William shared his condolences to the family of his former nanny after her stepson was killed in the New Year’s terror attack in New Orleans.
item.dc:creator: Sophia Vento, The Hill

item.title: Austrian Chancellor Nehammer says he will resign after talks on forming a new government fail
item.description: Austrian Chancellor Karl Nehammer says he will resign in the coming days after talks on forming a new government failed a second time.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: The last high-profile trip to India by the Biden administration will focus on technology
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — National security adviser Jake Sullivan will travel to India for a visit focusing on cooperation on strategic technology, including in defense, space and artificial intelligence.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump announces Middle East appointment: Hopefully she's learned her lesson
item.description: President-elect Trump tapped Morgan Ortagus, a former State Department spokesperson, to serve as deputy special presidential envoy for Middle East peace in his next administration. Trump noted in his announcement that Ortagus previously was critical of him.
item.dc:creator: Alex Gangitano, The Hill

item.title: Greenland leader calls for independence from Denmark as Trump enters
item.description: Greenland Prime Minister Múte Egede announced a desire to obtain independence from Denmark in the wake of President-elect Trump’s comments about buying the island territory.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, The Hill

item.title: Jake Sullivan, Biden discussed possibility of hitting Iran nuclear program: report
item.description: President Biden was presented scenario and response options on how to handle Iran should it make advances in developing a nuclear weapon, though the president has not signed off on hitting Tehran's program.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trump blasts UK energy policy and ‘windmills’ after company blames windfall tax for less drilling
item.description: President-elect Trump is criticizing the United Kingdom’s energy policy and saying it should get rid of windmills after a company blamed the country’s windfall tax for its early exit from drilling in Europe’s North Sea. In a post on his social media site Truth Social, Trump wrote, “The U.K.
item.dc:creator: Rachel Frazin, The Hill

item.title: South Korea's impeached president defies warrant after hourslong standoff
item.description: SEOUL, South Korea (AP) — South Korean investigators left the official residence of impeached President Yoon Suk Yeol after a near-six-hour standoff on Friday during which he defied their attempt to detain him.
item.dc:creator: KIM TONG-HYUNG, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: ISIS increasingly unopposed following US withdrawal from Afghanistan, collapse of Syria
item.description: The Islamic State is seeing less resistance in areas of major security concern following the U.S. withdrawal from Afghanistan, the fall of the Assad regime in Syria and potential troop drawdown in Iraq.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: New Zealand police officer killed in New Year's Day vehicle attack, another injured
item.description: A driver ran down two New Zealand police officers on patrol in the early hours of New Year’s Day, killing one and seriously injuring the other, the country’s police chief said.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Protesters in Syria demand justice for disappeared activists and accountability from all factions
item.description: DOUMA, Syria (AP) — Protesters in Syria held a sit-in Wednesday demanding justice for four activists who were forcibly disappeared in 2013 and whose fate remains one of the most haunting mysteries of the country's 13-year civil war. On Dec.
item.dc:creator: OMAR ALBAM and SALLY ABOU ALJOUD, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Israeli strikes kill 12 in Gaza, including children, as war grinds into the new year
item.description: DEIR AL-BALAH, Gaza Strip (AP) — Israeli strikes killed at least 12 Palestinians in the Gaza Strip, mostly women and children, officials said Wednesday, as the nearly 15-month war ground on into the new year.
item.dc:creator: WAFAA SHURAFA and SAMY MAGDY, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Jocelyne Wildenstein, Swiss socialite known for extreme plastic surgeries, dies at 84: reports
item.description: Wildenstein's facial surgeries were notably feline, leading the press to (cruelly) dub her "Catwoman.
item.dc:creator: Russell Falcon, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Pope calls for commitment to protect life as he doubles down on abortion in New Year's Day message
item.description: ROME (AP) — Pope Francis ushered in the New Year with a renewed appeal for the faithful to reject abortion, calling for a “firm commitment” to protect and respect life from conception to natural death. Francis, 88, celebrated a New Year’s Day Mass in St.
item.dc:creator: NICOLE WINFIELD, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: South Korea air crash investigators extract black box data as grieving families mourn the victims
item.description: SEOUL, South Korea (AP) — Grieving relatives of the victims of the South Korea plane crash gathered at the site to pay respects to their loved ones on New Year’s Day, as officials said they've extracted data from one of the retrieved black boxes to find the exact cause of the crash.
item.dc:creator: HYUNG-JIN KIM, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Netanyahu goes against doctor’s orders, appears in Israeli parliament after surgery
item.description: Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu appeared in the Israeli parliament on Tuesday for a crucial budget-related vote, two days after prostate surgery.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Ukraine: How the war shifted in 2024
item.description: A third year of war in Ukraine drags on as the new year and the conflict's February anniversary approach. The war in 2024 saw major shifts on the battlefield and in Western politics.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Court issues arrest warrant for South Korea's President Yoon
item.description: A South Korean court issues arrest warrant for sitting President Yoon Suk Yeol over his decision in December to impose martial law over the nation.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: National Guard soldier dies days after Christmas in 'non-combat' related incident in Iraq
item.description: An Indiana National Guard soldier deployed to Iraq in support of Operation Inherent Resolve died Saturday in a non-combat incident, Defense Department officials announced Monday.
item.dc:creator: Fox News
